core.utils.debug module

DEBUG UTILITY - Unified debugging across all A-LEMS modules

This module provides a consistent debugging interface. Debug output can be enabled globally or per-module via environment variables.

Usage in your code:

from core.utils.debug import dprint, set_debug

dprint(“Reading MSR…”, msr=0x60d, value=12345) # Only prints if debug on

Environment variables:

export A_LEMS_DEBUG=1 # Enable ALL debug output export A_LEMS_DEBUG_MODULES=msr_reader,rapl_reader # Specific modules only export A_LEMS_DEBUG_FILE=/tmp/a-lems-debug.log # Log to file (optional)

Author: Deepak Panigrahy

class core.utils.debug.Colors[source]

Bases: object

BLUE = '\x1b[94m'
BOLD = '\x1b[1m'
CYAN = '\x1b[96m'
DIM = '\x1b[2m'
GREEN = '\x1b[92m'
MAGENTA = '\x1b[95m'
RED = '\x1b[91m'
RESET = '\x1b[0m'
YELLOW = '\x1b[93m'
core.utils.debug.dprint(*args, **kwargs) → None[source]

Debug print – only prints if debug is enabled for current module.

Usage:

dprint(“Reading MSR…”) # Simple message dprint(“Value:”, 12345) # Multiple args dprint(“Counters:”, c2=100, c3=200) # Keyword args become key=value

Examples

dprint(“MSR read failed”, msr=0x60d, error=”Timeout”) dprint(“C-state counters:”, **counters)

core.utils.debug.dprint_raw(module: str, *args, **kwargs) → None[source]

Raw debug print – bypasses module check. Use for internal debug messages.

core.utils.debug.init_debug_from_env() → None[source]

Initialize debug settings from environment variables. Call this once at program start.

core.utils.debug.is_debug_enabled(module_name: str | None = None) → bool[source]

Check if debug is enabled for given module.

Parameters:

module_name – Module name (auto-detected if None)

Returns:

True if debug should be printed

core.utils.debug.set_debug(enabled: bool = True, module: str | None = None) → None[source]

Enable/disable debug for current module programmatically.

Parameters:

  • enabled – True to enable, False to disable

  • module – Module name (auto-detected if None)

core.utils.debug.trace(func)[source]

Decorator to trace function calls with debug output.

Usage:

@trace def my_function(x, y):

return x + y
